root_cause: Bandwidth saturation on CE-B1 wan0 (100 Mbps link at 94% utilization during peak hours)
resolution: Applied QoS policy to prioritize critical traffic, shaped non-critical flows. Scheduled link upgrade to 200 Mbps.

root_cause: BGP session to upstream provider dropped due to hold timer expiry; route flap damping triggered
resolution: Reset BGP session with soft refresh. Increased hold timer from 120s to 180s. Verified with provider no ACL blocking port 179.

root_cause: Optical interface degradation on P-1 eth2 (core backbone link to P-2). CRC errors caused LDP session flapping.
resolution: Traffic re-routed via P-1 eth3 (backup path). SFP replaced on P-1 eth2. LDP sessions re-established.

root_cause: Incorrect route-target import statement on PE-2 VRF 'CUSTOMER-A' caused route leak from production VRF
resolution: Corrected RT import under VRF. Withdrawn leaked prefixes using 'withdraw route' command. Committed config with 'commit confirmed'.

root_cause: Traffic engineering reroute placed additional LSPs through P-1 after P-2 maintenance
resolution: Identified maintenance window overlap. Returned P-2 to service. LSPs redistributed.
